chunk_summary: This section discusses diagnostic evaluation of abnormal growth using WHO/IAP charts, focusing on pediatric obesity staging, comorbidity screening, and tailored interventions for Indian populations.
learning_objectives: Understand the use of WHO/IAP growth charts for diagnosing abnormal growth in children.
Learn criteria for classifying overweight and obesity using Z-scores and BMI-for-age standards.
Identify comorbidities and red flags associated with pediatric obesity for targeted evaluation.
Explore culturally relevant counseling strategies for managing obesity in Indian children.
key_facts: Overweight is defined as weight-for-length/height >+2 SD (under 5 years) or BMI-for-age >+1 SD (5 years onward).
Obesity is classified as weight-for-length/height >+3 SD (under 5 years) or BMI-for-age >+2 SD (5 years onward).
Edmonton Obesity Staging System for Pediatrics (EOSS-P) incorporates metabolic, mental health, functional, and social factors.
Baseline screening for persistent obesity includes fasting lipids, glucose, and liver enzymes (ALT).
Red flags for secondary obesity include rapid onset, hyperphagia, developmental delay, dysmorphism, or short stature.
reference_quote: Overweight and obesity are defined using age-appropriate references: under 5 years, weight-for-length/height >+2 SD indicates overweight and >+3 SD obesity on WHO standards; from 5 years onward, classify using BMI-for-age.
document_key_topics: WHO/IAP growth charts for abnormal growth
Pediatric obesity staging and comorbidity screening
Culturally tailored obesity counseling in India
